commands.author: bkudiess
commands.command: re-review â€” addressed both p1 blockers:

target.title: Add gateway protocol client foundation (Mac/main parity, lane 1)
target.body:
## Summary

PR lane 1 of the Mac/main parity effort: adds the **typed gateway protocol client foundation** to the Windows node. The upstream OpenClaw gateway exposes a richer protocol that the Mac app already consumes; Windows previously consumed only a small subset. This PR adds strongly-typed DTOs + client methods so downstream lanes (chat command catalog, session/run controls, workspace file rail, compaction-checkpoint UX, provider/model readiness) can build on a typed surface instead of hand-parsing JSON.

Client/DTO layer only â€” no UI is wired yet. Wire shapes were verified field-by-field against the canonical `openclaw/openclaw` schemas, and proven end-to-end with a real WebSocket round-trip (see **Proof**).

## What's changing

### New typed APIs (on `OpenClawGatewayClient` + `IOperatorGatewayClient`)
| API | Gateway method | Purpose |
|-----|----------------|---------|
| `ListCommandsAsync(CommandCatalogQuery?)` | `commands.list` | Command catalog (name, nativeName, textAliases, description, category, source, scope, acceptsArgs, typed args with `{value,label}` choices + dynamic flags). Filtering is client-side; facets derived. |
| `PatchSessionAsync(key, SessionPatch)` | `sessions.patch` | Extended per-session settings (model, thinking/verbose/trace/reasoning/elevated levels, fastMode, responseUsage, exec host/security/ask/node, send policy, group activation). |
| `ListSessionFilesAsync(key, path?, search?)` / `GetSessionFileAsync(key, path)` | `sessions.files.list` / `get` | Files referenced by a session + folder browser, for a workspace file rail. |
| `ListCompactionCheckpointsAsync` / `GetCompactionCheckpointAsync` / `BranchCompactionCheckpointAsync` / `RestoreCompactionCheckpointAsync` | `sessions.compaction.*` | List/get/branch/restore compaction checkpoints. |

### Tri-state `SessionPatch` (clear support)
`SessionPatch` fields use a new `PatchField<T>` so each setting is **unset / set / clear**:
```csharp
patch.Model = "gpt-5";            // set    â†’ "model":"gpt-5"
patch.Model = SessionPatch.Clear; // clear  â†’ "model":null  (removes the session override)
// untouched                      // unset  â†’ omitted
patch.Model = null;               // unset  (null reference = leave alone, NOT clear)
```
The upstream schema types every patch field as `Union([<value>, Null])`, where explicit `null` clears an override â€” this matches Mac/main behavior and unblocks downstream "Default (clear)" menu actions. Existing assignments compile unchanged via implicit conversion. A blank string value is omitted (the strict gateway rejects `""`); clearing always sends a real JSON `null`.

### Behavior / compatibility
- **Backwards compatible (old gateways):** an "unknown method" error returns a typed result with `IsSupported = false` (logged), never thrown. Genuine protocol errors are **not** swallowed â€” read methods propagate them; mutation methods surface them in `result.Error`.
- **Backwards compatible (interface):** the 8 new `IOperatorGatewayClient` members are **default interface methods** (graceful `IsSupported=false` defaults), so existing external implementers / test doubles do not source-break. A reflection test asserts they are non-abstract.
- `OpenClawGatewayClient` was made `partial` (1 line) so new methods live in `OpenClawGatewayClient.Protocol.cs`; no existing behavior changed. The legacy `PatchSessionAsync(key, model:, â€¦)` overload is untouched.

## Files changed (7 files, +2146/-1)
| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `src/OpenClaw.Shared/GatewayProtocolModels.cs` | **new** â€” DTOs, enums, `PatchField<T>` tri-state |
| `src/OpenClaw.Shared/OpenClawGatewayClient.Protocol.cs` | **new** â€” client methods + `internal static` parsers |
| `src/OpenClaw.Shared/OpenClawGatewayClient.cs` | `class` â†’ `partial class` (1 line) |
| `src/OpenClaw.Shared/IOperatorGatewayClient.cs` | +8 default-method API declarations |
| `tests/OpenClaw.Shared.Tests/GatewayProtocolModelsTests.cs` | **new** â€” parser / payload / tri-state / DIM-compat tests |
| `tests/OpenClaw.Shared.Tests/GatewayProtocolLiveRoundTripTests.cs` | **new** â€” real loopback-WebSocket round-trip proof |
| `tests/OpenClaw.Tray.Tests/OnboardingChatBootstrapperTests.cs` | fake interface stubs |

## Validation
- `./build.ps1` â€” all 5 projects build âś…
- `OpenClaw.Shared.Tests` â€” **2395 passed**, 29 skipped âś…
- `OpenClaw.Tray.Tests` â€” **1123 passed** âś…
- Two-round adversarial reviews (GPT-5.5 + Claude Opus) per change; all findings addressed, including critical wire-contract corrections verified against upstream source.

## Proof â€” real WebSocket round-trip

`GatewayProtocolLiveRoundTripTests` connects the **real** `OpenClawGatewayClient` over a **real loopback WebSocket** to a stub gateway, invokes each new method, and asserts the exact JSON frames the client serializes onto the wire (then feeds back schema-shaped responses and asserts the typed DTOs parse). This exercises the full `method â†’ SerializeRequest â†’ socket send â†’ receive â†’ response â†’ parse â†’ typed DTO` stack â€” not crafted payloads.

Captured request frames (from the passing test's output):
```
{"type":"req","id":"â€¦","method":"commands.list"}
{"type":"req","id":"â€¦","method":"sessions.files.get","params":{"sessionKey":"agent:main:main","path":"src/a.cs"}}
{"type":"req","id":"â€¦","method":"sessions.compaction.list","params":{"key":"agent:main:main"}}
{"type":"req","id":"â€¦","method":"sessions.compaction.branch","params":{"key":"agent:main:main","checkpointId":"cp1"}}
{"type":"req","id":"â€¦","method":"sessions.patch","params":{"key":"agent:main:main","model":"gpt-5","fastMode":"auto"}}
{"type":"req","id":"â€¦","method":"sessions.patch","params":{"key":"agent:main:main","model":null}}
```
This proves the on-the-wire contract our client emits â€” `sessionKey` for files, `key`+`checkpointId` for compaction, and the tri-state `sessions.patch` `model:"gpt-5"` (set) vs `model:null` (clear) â€” matches the upstream `openclaw/openclaw` `packages/gateway-protocol/src/schema/{sessions,commands}.ts` schemas.

> Note: this is a loopback harness capturing the real client frames + schema-shaped parse (CI-durable). A capture against a live paired production gateway can be added if a gateway endpoint/token is available; the wire frames above are exactly what such a gateway receives.

## Risks / known limitations
- Contract is verified-but-external: if upstream renames a field/method, the typed layer needs updating. A `ToPayload` wire-name contract test fails on drift.
- Old-gateway detection hinges on the existing `IsUnknownMethodError` substring match (`"unknown method"`).
- No in-app consumer yet â€” dependent lanes will wire the UI.
- `sessions.patch` rejection surfaces on the existing `SessionCommandCompleted` event, not the `Task<bool>` (matches the legacy patch path).

đź¤– Drafted with assistance from Copilot CLI.
target.author: bkudiess
